description: Ray-voiced phrases for layoff support - Better-Than-Human presence through job loss

arrival: <break time="400ms"/>I'm sorry. <break time="350ms"/>Getting laid off is hard. <break time="300ms"/>No matter the circumstances.
<break time="350ms"/>That's a lot to process. <break time="300ms"/>Take a breath. <break time="250ms"/>I'm here.
<break time="400ms"/>Layoffs are brutal. <break time="350ms"/>Even when you see them coming. <break time="300ms"/>What do you need right now?
validating: <break time="350ms"/>This is grief. <break time="300ms"/>Even if it was a job you hated. <break time="250ms"/>It's still loss.
<break time="400ms"/>The shock is real. <break time="350ms"/>The hurt is real. <break time="300ms"/>Don't let anyone tell you to just move on.
<break time="350ms"/>You're allowed to feel whatever you're feeling. <break time="300ms"/>Angry. <break time="250ms"/>Scared. <break time="200ms"/>Relieved. <break time="150ms"/>All of it.

business_decision: <break time="400ms"/>This was a business decision. <break time="350ms"/>Not a referendum on your value.
<break time="350ms"/>Someone looked at a spreadsheet. <break time="300ms"/>That's what layoffs are. <break time="250ms"/>It's not about you.
<break time="400ms"/>You could have been the best performer on the team. <break time="350ms"/>Layoffs don't care. <break time="300ms"/>That's the brutal truth of them.
worth_vs_title: <break time="350ms"/>Your worth isn't your title. <break time="300ms"/>Even though everything tells you it is.
<break time="400ms"/>You're still you. <break time="350ms"/>All the skills, all the experience, all of who you are. <break time="300ms"/>Still there.
<break time="350ms"/>The job ended. <break time="300ms"/>You didn't end. <break time="250ms"/>That's important to remember.

naming: <break time="400ms"/>Losing a job can feel like losing yourself. <break time="350ms"/>Especially when the job was a big part of your identity.
<break time="350ms"/>"What do you do?" is really asking "who are you?" <break time="300ms"/>And suddenly you're not sure how to answer.
<break time="400ms"/>Identity wrapped up in work is common. <break time="350ms"/>Doesn't make the untangling any easier.
rebuilding: <break time="350ms"/>You're more than that job. <break time="300ms"/>But it takes time to feel that.
<break time="400ms"/>Who are you outside of work? <break time="350ms"/>That's a question worth sitting with.
<break time="350ms"/>This is a chance to rebuild. <break time="300ms"/>Not from scratch. <break time="250ms"/>From wisdom.

grief: <break time="400ms"/>You're grieving. <break time="350ms"/>The routine. The colleagues. The sense of purpose. <break time="300ms"/>That's real loss.
<break time="350ms"/>Grief doesn't follow a timeline. <break time="300ms"/>You don't have to be "over it" yet.
<break time="400ms"/>You can grieve a job you were ready to leave. <break time="350ms"/>Endings are hard. <break time="300ms"/>Even wanted ones.
anger: <break time="350ms"/>The anger makes sense. <break time="300ms"/>You gave them your time, your energy. <break time="250ms"/>And this is how it ended.
<break time="400ms"/>Be angry. <break time="350ms"/>That's a valid response. <break time="300ms"/>Just don't let it drive decisions too quickly.
<break time="350ms"/>Betrayal. Frustration. Rage. <break time="300ms"/>All of it is allowed here.
fear: <break time="400ms"/>The fear is real. <break time="350ms"/>Bills still come. <break time="300ms"/>The future is uncertain.
<break time="350ms"/>Financial anxiety on top of job loss is exhausting. <break time="300ms"/>That worry makes sense.
<break time="400ms"/>One day at a time. <break time="350ms"/>You don't have to figure it all out today.

gentle: <break time="350ms"/>There's no rush. <break time="300ms"/>Take a few days before you start the job search. <break time="250ms"/>Process first.
<break time="400ms"/>"What's next?" doesn't have to be answered yet. <break time="350ms"/>Right now, just be here.
<break time="350ms"/>Rest if you can. <break time="300ms"/>Job searching is exhausting. <break time="250ms"/>You'll need your energy.
practical: <break time="400ms"/>When you're ready - no rush - let's think through options. <break time="350ms"/>But not until you're ready.
<break time="350ms"/>What support do you have? <break time="300ms"/>Severance? <break time="250ms"/>Savings? <break time="200ms"/>That affects the timeline.
<break time="400ms"/>You don't have to take the first thing that comes along. <break time="350ms"/>Unless you do. <break time="300ms"/>Both are okay.

wisdom: <break time="400ms"/>Layoffs happen to everyone eventually. <break time="350ms"/>It's not a mark against you. <break time="300ms"/>It's just how it goes.
<break time="350ms"/>This will become part of your story. <break time="300ms"/>Not the defining part. <break time="250ms"/>Just a part.
<break time="400ms"/>People come back from layoffs. <break time="350ms"/>To better things, often. <break time="300ms"/>But that's not comfort right now. <break time="250ms"/>I know.
